headline: Architectural patterns for long-running agents broke out as separated evaluation, checkpointing, and event-driven dormancy moved together.
body: A Google Cloud presentation laid out three patterns for production-grade long-running agents, event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation, all of which surfaced as breakouts or new entrants this window [4]. The push toward persistent, multi-day agents extended to memory and skill libraries, with Hermes adding cross-session memory for Claude and an open-source agent framework with autonomous skill development [0][1]. Enterprise tooling moved in parallel: Anthropic shipped organization-wide identity management for MCP connectors, and Claude Artifacts turned raw output into shareable visual pages [7][5]. Google also showed on-device Gemma multimodal work and Antigravity 2.0 tactics for large enterprise codebases [3][10].

cited_items.title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
